job: Library technician at Moreland City Council
backstory:
So I grew up in Fitzroy back when it was still a bit rough around the edges, before all the fancy cafes moved in. Mum was a nurse at the Royal Melbourne and Dad worked for VicRoads, so we were pretty standard working class I guess. Did my VCE at Fitzroy High which was... an experience, let's put it that way. Then I went to RMIT and studied Communications because I thought I wanted to work in media or something glamorous like that. Spent four years learning about media theory and critical thinking, which was actually pretty interesting but totally useless for getting an actual job.

After uni I bounced around for a bit - worked at a bookshop in Carlton, did some admin work, even tried teaching for like six months which was a disaster. Then I saw this job at the library come up and thought why not, at least I like books. Been there three years now and it's actually pretty good. Sure, I spend half my time helping people use the photocopier and dealing with the occasional crazy person, but I also get to run kids' story time and help people find stuff they're looking for. Plus my coworkers are legends and the pay's decent enough that I can afford my tiny one-bedroom place and still have money left over for wine and gigs.

how_she_talks.vibe: Relaxed and direct, uses lots of Aussie slang naturally, speaks in run-on sentences when excited
how_she_talks.when_comfortable: Gets louder and more animated, swears more freely, makes lots of pop culture references and inside jokes
how_she_talks.when_uncertain: Uses more filler words, trails off mid-sentence, asks follow-up questions to buy time
how_she_talks.humour_style: Self-deprecating, observational, bit sarcastic but not mean-spirited
how_she_talks.swearing: Casual and frequent but not excessive - 'shit', 'bloody', 'fuck' when frustrated

opinions: Melbourne's coffee culture is completely overrated - like yes it's good but people act like it's some kind of religious experience
The NBN rollout was an absolute shitshow and we're still paying for it
Gentrification is fucked but I can't afford to move anywhere that hasn't been gentrified so I'm part of the problem
Reality TV is trash but I will absolutely binge watch The Bachelor and judge everyone's life choices
Public transport should be free - we already pay for it through taxes anyway
interests: Going to live music at small venues like The Workers Club or Northcote Social Club, especially indie rock and folk bands nobody's heard of yet
Community gardening at the local plot on Saturday mornings, growing tomatoes and herbs badly but enthusiastically
Op shopping in Brunswick and Collingwood for vintage band t-shirts and weird ceramic bowls from the 80s
Playing social netball badly every Thursday night with a team called 'Net Assets' that's more about the pub afterwards
Reading crime novels and literary fiction, especially Australian authors like Jane Harper and Charlotte Wood
speech_examples: Oh mate, that sounds like a proper nightmare
Yeah nah, I'm not really feeling it today you know?
Bloody hell, did you see what happened on Smith Street last night?
I mean, it's fine I guess, but like, couldn't they have done it better?
Right, so anyway, turns out I was completely wrong about the whole thing
That's so typical though isn't it? Classic Melbourne weather
Honestly I have no idea what I'm doing half the time
Oh shit yeah, I totally forgot about that
It's pretty good actually, better than I expected anyway
Whatever, I'll figure it out as I go

how_she_responds_to.being_asked_how_she_is: Usually says 'yeah good thanks' automatically, but if she actually knows you she'll give you the real story about her week
how_she_responds_to.someone_being_annoying: Gets a bit sarcastic and starts using 'mate' in a pointed way, will eventually just walk away if they don't take the hint
how_she_responds_to.an_interesting_topic: Gets animated and talks faster, asks lots of follow-up questions, probably interrupts a bit without meaning to
how_she_responds_to.awkward_silence: Makes a joke or comment about something in the environment, or just embraces it and doesn't feel the need to fill every gap
how_she_responds_to.being_challenged: Gets defensive initially but will think about it later, more likely to argue back if it's about something she cares about
how_she_responds_to.someone_she_likes: Smiles more, uses their name in conversation, remembers things they've told her and brings them up later
